## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `fix/quick-wins-quote-convert-alt-a`.

## Fix 1 — Quote PDF: remove redundant "Prepared by:" line

The quote PDF footer block currently shows:

```
If you have any queries regarding this quotation, please do not hesitate to contact:
Raj Pudasaini on 0421 140 248 or estimating@initialservices.net
All prices exclude GST. Add 10% if applicable.
This quote is valid for 30 days from the date of issue or the end of the current financial year, whichever is first.
Prepared by: Raj Pudasaini | estimating@initialservices.net
Payment terms: 25 days from date of invoice (BIFA compliant).
```

The "Prepared by:" line is redundant — the estimator's name and email already appear two lines above ("Raj Pudasaini on 0421 140 248 or estimating@initialservices.net").

**Fix:** find the quote PDF template (search the repo for `Prepared by` in the quote-render code — likely under `apps/api/src/modules/client-quotes/quote-pdf.*` or a Handlebars/EJS template under that directory). Remove the entire `Prepared by: …` line. Keep the contact line above and the payment-terms line below.

## Fix 2 — Convert-to-project gate: require CONTRACT_ISSUED, not AWARDED

Today the "Convert to project" button on the tender detail page becomes available when `tender.status === "AWARDED"`. The lifecycle is:
`DRAFT → IN_PROGRESS → SUBMITTED → AWARDED → CONTRACT_ISSUED → (convert to job)`

Conversion at AWARDED is premature — the client has confirmed we won but the contract hasn't been signed yet. It should only be available at CONTRACT_ISSUED.

**Fix:**
- In the tender detail UI (search for `convert-to-job` button or `canConvertToProject` predicate — likely in `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderDetailPage.tsx` or a sibling component), change the visibility/enabled gate from `status === "AWARDED"` to `status === "CONTRACT_ISSUED"`.
- In the API service that backs `POST /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/convert-to-job`, change the status precondition check from `AWARDED` to `CONTRACT_ISSUED`. Throw a `BadRequestException` with a clear message if attempted at any other status.
- Update any existing tests that assumed AWARDED was the trigger.

## Fix 3 — Alt+A: remove the input/textarea/contenteditable guard

PR #238 (floating Assumptions/Exclusions editor) added a keydown handler in `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/TenderDetailPage.tsx` (around lines 165–176) that listens for Alt+A and toggles the editor panel. It currently guards against firing when focus is inside an `INPUT`, `TEXTAREA`, or `contenteditable` element:

```ts
const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (!e.altKey || e.key.toLowerCase() !== "a") return;
  const tag = (e.target as HTMLElement)?.tagName;
  if (tag === "INPUT" || tag === "TEXTAREA" || (e.target as HTMLElement)?.isContentEditable) return;
  e.preventDefault();
  setAeEditorOpen((o) => !o);
};
```

Owner's clarification: Alt+A should toggle the panel **regardless of focus**, as long as the user is on a tab where the shortcut is active (Overview, Scope of Works — not Quote).

**Fix:** remove the input/textarea/contenteditable guard. The handler becomes:

```ts
const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (!e.altKey || e.key.toLowerCase() !== "a") return;
  e.preventDefault();
  setAeEditorOpen((o) => !o);
};
```

Keep the early-return on `tab === "quote"` (the effect dependency) and the cleanup `removeEventListener` on unmount.

Note: this means typing "a" while holding Alt anywhere in the page (including inside a description textarea) will toggle the panel. Owner is aware and wants this behaviour.

- Quote PDF: regenerate a sample quote PDF, confirm "Prepared by:" line is gone. Snapshot test if one exists for the quote PDF template, otherwise visual smoke.
- Convert gate: API test — try POST `/convert-to-job` on a tender with status AWARDED → expect 400; with status CONTRACT_ISSUED → expect 200/201. UI test — button hidden/disabled at AWARDED, visible at CONTRACT_ISSUED.
- Alt+A: simulate keydown with focus on a textarea, confirm panel toggles (it should now, where previously it didn't).
